---
title: "PyCryption Lab"
subtitle: "Specimens, composers, and benchmarks for encryption research"
toc: false
---

Welcome to the front desk. PyCryption is an encryption research lab built
around **composers** — harnesses that wrap encryption algorithms with
automatic metrics collection, so testing an idea costs minutes instead of an
afternoon of scaffolding.

## The Wings

| Wing | What's inside |
|---|---|
| [On-Ramp](General%20Cryptography.ipynb) | Cryptography fundamentals, level by level — from XOR to KEMs |
| [Composer Tutorial](Encryption%20Composer.ipynb) | Build, test, and benchmark a prototype algorithm against production AEADs |
| [Symmetric](Symmetric.ipynb) | Symmetric cipher benchmarks at scale |
| [Asymmetric](Asymmetric.ipynb) | Asymmetric primitives (in progress) |
| [ML-KEM](ML-KEM.ipynb) | Post-quantum hybrid encryption — ML-KEM-768 + AES-256-GCM (FIPS 203) |

## The Specimen Shelf

Production-grade implementations in `lib/algorithms`, all rideable through
the composer:

- **AES-256-GCM** — the block-cipher AEAD workhorse
- **ChaCha20-Poly1305** — RFC 8439 stream AEAD, Salsa20's modern descendant
- **ML-KEM-768 + AES-256-GCM** — post-quantum KEM-DEM hybrid

## Running the Lab

```bash
uv sync         # install dependencies
task            # list all lab operations
task test       # full test suite
task ride       # benchmark the specimen shelf in your terminal
```

Every result table on this site comes from committed notebook outputs —
the site build never executes code. Outputs are refreshed deliberately with
`task nb:exec`, so benchmarks are reviewable in git history like any other
change.
